BERITA POPULER: Perdagangan Emas Terguncang hingga Harga Emas Dunia dan Perhiasan
Iran Conflict Could Push Rupiah Toward Rp 20,000: Analyst
body: Escalating Iran tensions risk pushing the rupiah toward Rp 20,000 per USD as oil prices surge and capital outflows intensify.

title: From Trade Deal to Probes: Indonesia Avoids Suing US at WTO
body: Indonesia has had some legal battles against the US at the WTO in the past, but it now prefers to talk things over.

title: Heavy Trucks Barred from Roads as Indonesia Manages Post-Eid Travel Surge
body: Indonesia bans heavy trucks until March 29 to ease post-Eid traffic as millions return to Greater Jakarta.

title: Indonesia Says It Treats US, China as Equals
body: Indonesia sticks to its stance of not wanting to get involved in the US-China struggle for hegemony.

title: Stable Prices, Ample Food Supply Mark Ramadan–Eid Period: Trade Minister
body: Food prices remained stable and supplies sufficient during Ramadan–Eid 2026, with coordinated monitoring preventing spikes and shortages.

title: Businesses See Downside to Indonesia’s WFH Plan to Tackle Oil Shock
body: Businesses warn Indonesia’s WFH plan to cut fuel use may curb consumption and slow growth as oil prices surge past $100 per barrel.

title: Antam Gold Drops Rp 50,000 on Monday, Tracks Global Sell-Off
body: Antam gold fell Rp 50,000 to Rp 2.84 M as global prices dropped 4.56%, with strong dollar and high rates pressuring bullion.

title: Minister Warns of China’s Growing Grip on Indonesia's Digital Marketplaces
body: Indonesia considers measures to curb Chinese dominance in e-commerce and boost local products after ByteDance’s Tokopedia deal.

title: Indonesian Bank Stocks Hit Hard by Foreign Outflows Amid Iran Conflict
body: Indonesia’s major bank stocks fall as foreign investors pull out Rp 3.82 trillion amid escalating Iran conflict.

title: How Ironic for Cacao-Rich Indonesia to Import Chocolate
body: Prabowo finds it ironic that the cacao-rich Indonesia is still importing global brands such as Kit Kat and Cadbury.

title: Gold Under Pressure as Rate Outlook Shifts, Antam Gold Extends Slide
body: Antam gold fell to Rp 2.893M as global prices slid, with hawkish central bank signals and inflation fears weighing on demand.

title: Eid Pause in Free Meals Program Saves Up to Rp 5 T
body: Indonesia is set to save up to Rp 5 trillion after temporarily suspending its free meals program during Eid.

title: Prabowo Eyes Coal Windfall Levy to Keep Budget Deficit Below 3%
body: Indonesia considers a coal windfall tax and spending cuts to keep its budget deficit below the 3% of GDP legal limit.

title: BCA Reclaims Top Spot as Indonesia’s Most Valuable Listed Company
body: BCA regains top spot on IDX with Rp 827 trillion market cap, overtaking BREN as shares in both companies decline year to date.

title: Indonesians in South Korea Can Pay with QRIS Starting April
body: The QRIS expansion to South Korea is expected to be a boon for Indonesian travelers as tourist numbers hit a record-high in 2025.

title: New US-Indonesia Trade Deal Secures Fossil Fuels and Access to Critical Minerals
body: Indonesia has pledged to promote US investment across its mineral industry, from exploration and mining to refining, transport, and export.

title: Geo Dipa, Lilac Partner on Indonesia’s First Geothermal Lithium Project
body: Indonesia and US firms sign a deal to extract lithium from geothermal brine, supporting EV supply chains and clean energy development.

title: Indonesia Targets 13 GW Solar Rollout in Initial Phase of 100 GW Plan
body: Indonesia plans a 13 GW solar rollout in the first phase of a 100 GW target, aiming to accelerate renewable energy and expand rural access.

title: Fisheries Exports Plunge 41% as Iran Conflict Chokes Global Supply Chains
body: Indonesia’s fisheries exports fell sharply as Middle East tensions disrupted shipping and drove up logistics costs.

title: Budget Cuts Risk Dragging Growth if Done Blindly, Economists Warn
body: Economists warn aggressive, poorly targeted budget cuts could slow growth, urging smarter reallocations over across-the-board trimming.
